message:
fix(sync): normalize legacy Go-beads terminal statuses to Closed on import

Companion fix to the storage-layer v6 migration: pivot legacy status
aliases that arrive via JSONL import (rather than direct DB writes)
to the canonical `Status::Closed` before anything else touches the
issue. Without this, imported bd-style issues with `"done"` /
`"complete"` / `"completed"` / `"finished"` / `"resolved"` keep
round-tripping as `Status::Custom(raw)` and the CHECK constraint
rejects them (same root cause as the v6 migration, different code
path).

src/sync/mod.rs — `normalize_issue`:
- Before the existing content-hash recompute, add a small branch
  that inspects `issue.status`. If it's `Status::Custom(raw)` and
  the lowercase trimmed value matches the bd terminal-alias set
  (`done | complete | completed | finished | resolved`), replace
  it with `Status::Closed`. The existing "closed status needs a
  closed_at" repair then runs against the normalized state, so
  nothing else needs to change.
- Deliberately leave unknown Custom statuses alone — project teams
  legitimately use custom status strings like `"qa-review"`, and
  silently promoting those to Closed would destroy that signal.

Unit tests in the same file:
- `test_normalize_issue_remaps_legacy_done_to_closed` — start with
  `Status::Custom("done")` and `closed_at: None`; assert normalize
  produces Status::Closed AND populates closed_at (via the
  existing repair logic, which now fires because the status is
  properly terminal).
- `test_normalize_issue_remaps_mixed_case_terminal_aliases` —
  parameterize over `["Done", "COMPLETE", "completed", "Finished",
  "Resolved"]`; each must round-trip through normalize into
  Status::Closed. Lowercase + trim is the matching rule.
- `test_normalize_issue_preserves_unknown_custom_status` —
  `Status::Custom("qa-review")` survives unchanged. Locks in the
  "don't over-collapse custom statuses" behaviour.

tests/proptest_jsonl_roundtrip.rs:
- rustfmt reorders the `beads_rust::sync::{...}` use-group so
  `ExportConfig, ImportConfig` come before the function imports
  that reference them. No import set change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
